refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates.' High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and operator identity; (c) all updates are logged with before/after values, reason, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is unbroken and queryable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance targets as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, operator, safety margins).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, when, why, before/after).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Provide traceability from worksite inputs through derived requirements to baseline and history records.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters (via Tasking interface), applies deterministic algorithms to compute performance targets, records baselines in a persistent store, and logs all updates with full audit metadata.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation with Local Caching – Performance derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, with periodic synchronization to a central audit log; reduces latency but increases coupling and diffusion risk.

DP-C: Manual Baseline Entry with Post-Hoc Validation – Operators manually enter performance targets via UI; system validates against mission context and logs changes; simpler but error-prone and non-compliant with 'automatic derivation' requirement.

DP-D: Hybrid Derivation with Safety Services Oversight – Performance targets derived by a core engine, then validated and approved by Safety Services module before baseline recording; adds safety gate but increases latency.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters (via Tasking interface), applies deterministic algorithms to compute performance targets, records baselines in a persistent store, and logs all updates with full audit metadata.

potential_sources: Dependency on Tasking interface for mission context input – well-defined, external to this DP.
Dependency on persistent storage for baseline and history – standard architectural service, not a functional coupling.
No circular dependencies with Vehicle or Teleoperation logic; derivation is upstream of operational execution.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low functional coupling. The derivation engine is decoupled from vehicle control and teleoperation; it consumes mission context and produces performance targets that are consumed downstream. No feedback loops or bidirectional dependencies detected at this level.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is available via Tasking interface with sufficient fidelity (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, energy budget, availability SLA).
Assumption: Deterministic algorithms for performance target derivation are well-defined and validated for the NGHE domain.
Risk: If mission context is incomplete or arrives late, derivation may produce invalid or stale targets.
Risk: High safety criticality demands that derivation algorithms are formally verified; this DP assumes such verification is feasible.
Risk: Audit trail volume may grow large; storage and query performance must be managed.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend: (1) Explicitly define the derivation algorithm specification as a separate design artifact (e.g., performance target computation rules, safety margin policies). (2) Specify the Tasking interface contract (required mission context fields, update frequency, validation rules). (3) Define audit log schema and retention policy to manage storage growth.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary. It contains all subsystems and services, including Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and the Performance Derivation Engine. The Performance Derivation Engine is a logical component within NGHE Solution that owns the derivation, baseline recording, and history management functions.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, energy budget, availability SLA, safety constraints) from Site Manager or planning system. Used as input to performance target derivation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance targets, current baseline, and history metadata to monitoring and reporting systems. Enables Site Manager and Safety Manager to view performance requirements and audit trail.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and policies from Safety Services; outputs performance targets with embedded safety margins and alerts if derivation violates safety policies.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest containment. Performance derivation is a system-level concern that must be visible and managed at the NGHE Solution boundary. All inputs (Tasking, SafetyAlert) and outputs (Telemetry) are external to the solution. No diffusion risk; this is the natural home for the derivation engine.

Low containment. Vehicle is an operational execution module; embedding derivation logic here would couple mission planning to vehicle control, violate separation of concerns, and create diffusion if multiple vehicles must share the same performance targets. Not recommended.

Low containment. Teleoperation is a remote control interface; derivation logic is orthogonal to tele-operation. Embedding here would create tight coupling between mission planning and remote control, and diffusion if multiple tele-operators must access the same targets. Not recommended.

Moderate-low containment. Safety Services validates and enforces safety policies, but does not own the derivation of performance targets from mission context. Derivation is a planning/configuration function, not a safety enforcement function. Safety Services should validate the output of derivation, not own the derivation itself. Partial containment only if Safety Services is extended to include performance target validation; not recommended as primary owner.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Derivation Engine is best contained within the NGHE Solution module as a logical subsystem. It receives mission context via the Tasking interface, applies derivation algorithms, records baselines and history, and outputs targets via Telemetry. Safety Services validates the targets via SafetyAlert. This placement ensures: (1) single source of truth for performance targets, (2) clear separation of planning (derivation) from execution (Vehicle, Teleoperation), (3) centralized audit trail for compliance and traceability, (4) no diffusion across multiple modules.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is treated as a black box, the Performance Derivation Engine may be invisible to stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager) who need to audit and approve performance targets. Mitigation: Ensure Telemetry interface exposes derivation metadata and history.

Risk: If the derivation algorithm is not formally specified or validated, the engine may produce incorrect targets. Mitigation: Require algorithm specification and formal verification as part of DP design.
Risk: Audit trail volume may grow unbounded, impacting storage and query performance. Mitigation: Define retention policy and archival strategy.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign Performance Derivation Engine to NGHE Solution. Recommend: (1) Define a sub-module or logical component within NGHE Solution called 'Performance Derivation Engine' with clear ownership and interface contracts. (2) Specify the Tasking interface contract (mission context schema, update frequency). (3) Specify the Telemetry output schema (derived targets, baseline metadata, history records). (4) Define SafetyAlert validation rules and approval workflow. (5) Document the derivation algorithm specification and formal verification plan.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) derives targets from mission context via Tasking, (2) records baselines with metadata, (3) maintains audit trail of updates, (4) provides traceability. The DP is specific, implementable, and traceable to the refined FR. No ambiguity or missing mappings.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk. The Performance Derivation Engine is a cohesive logical unit with clear inputs (Tasking, SafetyAlert) and outputs (Telemetry). No duplication or scattering across multiple modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct container. The derivation engine is a system-level service that must be visible and managed at the solution boundary. All external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) are already defined and available. No new interfaces required.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible. The refined FR specifies testable criteria: (a) automatic derivation from mission inputs, (b) baseline recording with metadata, (c) audit trail with full history, (d) traceability chain. All can be verified via integration testing, audit log inspection, and traceability matrix.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear. The Performance Derivation Engine is allocated to NGHE Solution. Inputs (Tasking, SafetyAlert) and outputs (Telemetry) are allocated to known interfaces. No ambiguity or missing allocations.

recommended_revisions: Clarify the derivation algorithm specification: define the rules for computing cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, and safety margins from mission context. This should be a separate design artifact (e.g., performance target computation specification).

Define the Tasking interface contract: specify the mission context schema (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, energy budget, availability SLA, safety constraints), update frequency, and validation rules.

Define the Telemetry output schema: specify the structure of derived targets, baseline metadata (timestamp, source, operator, safety margins), and history records (update log with before/after values, reason, approver).
Define the SafetyAlert validation workflow: specify how Safety Services validates derived targets, approval criteria, and escalation procedures for non-compliant targets.
Define audit log retention and archival policy: specify how long history records are retained, archival strategy, and query performance requirements.

decision.justification: The refined FR is decomposable into four atomic sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through 005.4), each addressing a distinct functional concern: derivation, baseline recording, history management, and traceability. The selected DP (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) is sound and well-contained within NGHE Solution. However, the sub-FRs require further decomposition to specify the detailed design of the derivation algorithm, baseline storage schema, audit log structure, and traceability mechanisms. Proceeding to the next level will clarify the detailed design and identify any additional DP candidates or IM assignments needed.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance targets as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, operator, safety margins).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, when, why, before/after).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Provide traceability from worksite inputs through derived requirements to baseline and history records.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available via Tasking interface with sufficient fidelity (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, energy budget, availability SLA).
Deterministic algorithms for performance target derivation are well-defined and validated for the NGHE domain.
Safety Services module is available to validate derived targets against safety policies.
Persistent storage (database or file system) is available for baseline and history records.
Telemetry interface can accommodate performance target metadata and history records without performance degradation.
open_risks: Risk: If mission context is incomplete or arrives late, derivation may produce invalid or stale targets. Mitigation: Define validation rules and error handling for incomplete mission context.
Risk: High safety criticality demands that derivation algorithms are formally verified. Mitigation: Require formal specification and verification of derivation algorithms as part of detailed design.
Risk: Audit trail volume may grow large; storage and query performance must be managed. Mitigation: Define retention policy, archival strategy, and query optimization.
Risk: If derivation algorithm is not transparent to stakeholders, they may not trust the derived targets. Mitigation: Provide detailed documentation and traceability of derivation logic.
evidence_needed: Derivation algorithm specification: formal definition of rules for computing cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, and safety margins from mission context.
Tasking interface contract: mission context schema, update frequency, validation rules.
Telemetry output schema: structure of derived targets, baseline metadata, history records.
SafetyAlert validation workflow: approval criteria, escalation procedures.
Audit log retention and archival policy: retention period, archival strategy, query performance requirements.
Formal verification plan: approach for verifying derivation algorithms against safety requirements.
